title: cerastium triviale subsp. fontanum
intro: Cerastium triviale subsp. fontanum, also known as a type of toadflax or small toadflax, is a flowering plant species belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). Traditionally, Cerastium triviale subsp. fontanum has been used in herbal medicine due to its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and exhibiting antiseptic properties, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, Cerastium triviale subsp. fontanum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
